text: Canyonlands National Park
zh: 峡谷地国家公园

text: vast spectacular Canyonlands National Park
zh: 辽阔壮丽的峡谷地国家公园

text: West of the Colorado Plateau, the park is located in the southeastern part of Utah, an area rich in canyons.
zh: 公园位于科罗拉多高原以西,犹他州东南部,这里遍布峡谷。

text: At the confluence of the Colorado and Green Rivers, a mesa rises
zh: 在科罗拉多河与格林河的交汇处,一座台地拔地而起

text: from sheer sandstone Stone cliffs a thousand feet into the air, over the surrounding canyon lands panorama.
zh: 陡峭的砂岩峭壁高耸入云,足有千英尺,俯瞰着周围的峡谷全景。

text: The island in the sky. A 43 square mile wilderness mason.
zh: 天空之岛,一片43平方英里的荒野秘境。

text: Rugged. Harsh. Primitive.
zh: 崎岖、严酷、原始。

text: A maze of unexplored canyons.
zh: 这是一片未经探索的峡谷迷宫。

text: A winding labyrinth of hazardous chasms. One of the most inaccessible regions in the United States.
zh: 蜿蜒曲折、危机四伏的深壑,是美国最难以到达的地区之一。

text: Cross the Colorado River from the maze and you're in the needles.
zh: 越过科罗拉多河,从迷宫区来到针石区,

text: Wilderness. Massive spires of rust and coral standing in twisted formation.
zh: 这片荒野中,巨大的锈红与珊瑚色尖塔以扭曲的姿态耸立。

text: Solid rock patterned with red and white bands, which were 270 million years in the making.
zh: 坚硬的岩石上布满红白相间的条纹,这是两亿七千万年岁月的杰作。

text: Search the landforms of Needles and you'll come across ancient Indian ruins
zh: 探寻针石区的地貌,你会发现古老的印第安遗迹

text: and these prehistoric petroglyphs and pictographs.
zh: 以及这些史前岩刻与岩画。

text: Needles is also the site of the Cave Spring Trail. Here is where cowboys used to herd their cattle.
zh: 针石区还有洞泉小径,牛仔们曾在此放牧牛群。

text: Traces of what had been their camps are found here. in this cave.
zh: 这处洞穴里,还留有他们扎营的痕迹。

text: Cowboys no longer come to the cave to get water, but birds do.
zh: 牛仔们不再来这里取水,但鸟儿依然会来。

text: Nevertheless, this is cowboy country. Cowboys still tend cattle and acreage skirting the park.
zh: 尽管如此,这里仍是牛仔的地盘,牛仔们仍在公园周边的土地上放牧牛群。

text: Just outside the park is Robbers Roost, where the infamous Wild Bunch hit out.
zh: 公园外不远处便是强盗谷,臭名昭著的“野帮”曾在此藏身。

text: The Roost's most celebrated outlaws are Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid,
zh: 强盗谷最负盛名的两位亡命之徒,是布奇·卡西迪与圣丹斯小子,

text: best known as they were portrayed in a classic Hollywood western.
zh: 人们对他们的印象大多来自一部经典好莱坞西部片。

text: Those runaway outlaws have long since fled. But the bighorn sheep are still here, though very wary.
zh: 那些亡命之徒早已消失无踪,但大角羊依然生活在这里,只是格外警觉。

text: Their skittishness is understandable. Before park protection, they'd been hunted almost to extinction.
zh: 它们的谨慎不难理解:在公园保护之前,它们几乎被猎杀殆尽。

text: It's simply amazing, their ability to skitter on the narrowest of ledges at the highest of heights.
zh: 令人惊叹的是,它们竟能在最窄的岩架、最高的悬崖上灵活穿行。

text: While hiking, should you stumble on remote Horseshoe Canyon,
zh: 徒步途中,如果你偶然闯入偏僻的马蹄峡谷,

text: you'll encounter a great gallery of prehistoric rock art, one of the most significant in North America.
zh: 你会看到一座宏大的史前岩画长廊,堪称北美最重要的岩画遗址之一。

text: Sinister, faceless, bigger -than -life figures hovering over you.
zh: 那些阴森、无脸、体形巨大的人物形象,仿佛悬浮在你头顶。

text: Taller than humans, there are those who believe that they are pictures of one -time visitors from outer space.
zh: 它们高于常人,有人相信这些是外星访客留下的形象。

text: More likely, ritual rock art creations of nomadic archaic people who lived in these canyons several thousand years ago.
zh: 但更可能的说法是,这是数千年前生活在这些峡谷中的游牧古人所创作的祭祀岩画。

text: Where the two rivers collide, the Colorado and the Green,
zh: 科罗拉多河与格林河在此交汇,

text: the rivers that carve these canyons, they're transformed into raging white water.
zh: 这两条雕刻出峡谷的河流,在此化作汹涌的激流。

text: Rafters need permits to run the course, with a skilled river runner in charge.
zh: 漂流者需持有许可证方可通行,并由经验丰富的领航员带队。

text: Here, the waters plunge 30 feet in just one mile.
zh: 在这里,水流仅一英里便骤降三十英尺。

text: A jeep road over Elephant Hill is the most challenging trailway in the park. You need a four -wheel drive vehicle to climb it.
zh: 穿越象山的吉普路线是公园内最具挑战性的道路,必须驾驶四驱车才能攀爬。

text: Here, you'll see plenty of tricked -out cars chugging noisily up this rock -strewn slope.
zh: 在这里,你会看到许多经过改装的车辆,轰鸣着艰难爬上这条布满碎石的斜坡。

text: sportier and increasingly popular way to negotiate these tortuous dirt roads is mountain biking.
zh: 更具运动感、也日益流行的穿越这些崎岖土路的方式,是山地自行车。

text: Stand on a park overlook and gasp at the spectacle before you.
zh: 站在公园的观景台上,眼前的景象定会令你屏息。

text: Canyon after canyon reaching to the horizon.
zh: 峡谷层层叠叠,一直延伸到天际。

text: This isn't simply the Wild West. It's America at its wildest.
zh: 这里不只是狂野西部,更是美国最狂野的模样。
